_meta: Initial learned rules distilled at build time from the 61 human corrections. In production these are proposed by Sonnet from aggregated feedback, deduped, decayed, and each must pass the eval gate before going active. Values here are the bootstrap set.

rules.rule_text: A US work-authorization / W-2 / 'no 1099' / US-citizen requirement is NOT a disqualifier for a staffing seller — these are normal US employers and are the target customers. Treat as an outreach flag (convince them to hire abroad), not a penalty.
rules.pattern: must be authorized to work in the (US|United States)|W-?2 only|no (contractors|1099)|US citizen|US-based

rules.rule_text: Senior/leadership titles (Sr., Senior, Lead, Analyst, Director, Manager, 'Strategic', 'Executive', 'Success Specialist') are not fillable by a junior VA — disqualify or hard-cap.

Roles requiring US coding certifications (CPC, CCS, CPB, RHIA, AAPC/AHIMA, EPIC-certified, 'Medical Coder', 'Medical Coding Specialist') are hard for an offshore VA to fill — deprioritize (cap ~60), do not hard-drop.
Postings by staffing/recruiting/intermediary companies are lower value (competitor/reseller) — deprioritize, do not drop (some BPOs are still good).
Full-time salaried roles (vs our hourly model) are still leads — flag for the 'hourly cost-comparison' outreach angle, never a disqualifier.
Expired / 'no longer accepting applications' postings should be dropped — needs a data-freshness signal (posting age / status), not text alone.
Very low posted salary (roughly under ~$16/hr or under ~$35k/yr) is harder to convince to switch to a VA — annotate for outreach, minor deprioritize only.
